reasoning: A well-constructed CBC padding oracle with two genuine hardening layers: IV-dependent dynamic padding mask (XOR with iv[0]^0x3C) and HMAC-based oracle obfuscation that replaces traditional distinct HTTP status codes with keyed signatures varying by error state and first-plaintext-byte context. The attacker must reverse-engineer the non-standard padding scheme, distinguish HMAC traces to build the oracle, and adapt a standard padding oracle attack — standard tools won't work out of the box. Flag isolation is strong (build-time encryption, file deletion, privilege drop). Note: the intended solution description references HTTP 400/422 status codes, but the actual implementation uses uniform 200 responses with HMAC traces, which is more sophisticated than described.
